codeReview.summary: The `parentJid` plumbing is wired through the shell stack, but the bridge still does not persist the inherited model onto the spawned `RegisteredScoop`, so the real `ScoopContext` path continues to fall back to `resolveCurrentModel()` when no explicit `--model` override is given. The new inheritance tests only cover mocked `createContext` seams, so the original blocking issue remains.

issues.description: `spawn()` computes `effectiveModelId` from `parentJid` at `packages/webapp/src/scoops/agent-bridge.ts:186-187`, but it only writes `scoop.config.modelId` when `requestedModelId !== undefined` at `:220`. The default `createContext` path at `:270-276` ignores `args.modelId` and constructs `new ScoopContext(args.scoop, ...)`, and `packages/webapp/src/scoops/scoop-context.ts:242-244` resolves the running model from `this.scoop.config.modelId` or `resolveCurrentModel()`. So a no-override spawn from a parent scoop/cone still uses the global UI model in production, which is the original blocking failure. The feature's new inheritance tests at `packages/webapp/tests/scoops/agent-bridge.test.ts:951-1110` and `packages/webapp/tests/scoops/agent-integration.test.ts:636-752` only assert the mocked `createContext` seam, while the only real-`ScoopContext` scenario at `packages/webapp/tests/scoops/agent-integration.test.ts:1101-1123` covers only a generic happy path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `webapp-worker` skill already requires a real `ScoopContext` test path for model-inheritance claims, but this feature's handoff still marked the procedure as followed after adding only mock-`createContext` inheritance coverage.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/webapp-worker/SKILL.md:75-80` requires a real `new ScoopContext(...)` path for model inheritance. The worker handoff at `/Users/trieloff/.factory/missions/094c0314-6896-4f76-bf58-89b7dfee66f4/handoffs/2026-04-16T21-32-35-886Z__fix-agent-bridge-model-inheritance__c8cf7352-fefc-4f3c-9b1a-142124325098.json:11-12,108-110` describes only mock-provider/createContext inheritance tests and still reports `followedProcedure: true`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission architecture note for the bridge is stale: it still documents `spawn({ cwd, allowedCommands, prompt, modelId? })` and the old data flow, but does not capture the new `parentJid` contract or the critical fact that the real `ScoopContext` resolves models from `scoop.config.modelId`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md:29-35,75-81` still shows the pre-fix `spawn` signature/data flow with no `parentJid`. The production behavior actually depends on persisting the model onto the `RegisteredScoop` (`packages/webapp/src/scoops/agent-bridge.ts:219-220`) because `packages/webapp/src/scoops/scoop-context.ts:242-244` reads `this.scoop.config.modelId`, not `AgentBridgeContextArgs.modelId`.

summary: Fail: the fix plumbs `parentJid` through the shell stack, but it still leaves inherited models off the spawned scoop record, so the real `ScoopContext` path keeps falling back to the global UI model when no override is provided. The added tests only validate the mocked bridge/context seam, so the original blocking issue is still unresolved.
